heading: Extension of time for payment of estate tax on value of reversionary or remainder interest in property
label: 6163 Extension of time for payment of estate tax on value of reversionary or remainder interest in property
notes: Amendments1976—Subsec. (a). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)Subsec. (b). , substituted provisions relating to extension of time for payment for a reasonable cause for provisions relating to extension of time for payment for undue hardship to the estate.Pub. L. 94–455, § 2004(c)(3)Subsec. (c). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)1975—Subsec. (c). struck out par. (b) and redesignated former subsec. (b) as (c).Pub. L. 85–866

Effective Date of 1976 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to estates of decedents dying after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .section 2004(c)(3) of Pub. L. 94–455section 2004(g) of Pub. L. 94–455section 6166 of this titleDec. 31, 1976

Effective Date of 1975 AmendmentAmendment by effective on , and applicable to amounts outstanding on such date or arising thereafter, see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 93–625section 7(e) of Pub. L. 93–625section 6621 of this titleJuly 1, 1975

Effective Date of 1964 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 88–272, title II, § 240(c)78 Stat. 129Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Feb. 26, 1964Oct. 22, 1986“(1)The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply in the case of any reversionary or remainder interest only if the time for payment of the tax under chapter 11 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] attributable to such interest, including any extensions thereof, has not expired on the date of the enactment of this Act [].Feb. 26, 1964“(2)The amendment made by subsection (b) [amending section 925 of I.R.C. 1939] shall apply in the case of any reversionary or remainder interest only if the time for payment of the tax under chapter 3 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1939 attributable to such interest, including any extensions thereof, has not expired on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Feb. 26, 1964

Effective Date of 1958 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 85–866, title I, § 66(b)(3)72 Stat. 1658Sept. 2, 1958“The amendments made by paragraphs (1) and (2) [amending this section and sections 925 and 926 of I.R.C. 1939] shall apply in the case of any reversionary or remainder interest only if the precedent interest or interests in the property did not terminate before the beginning of the 6-month period which ends on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Sept. 2, 1958

children.heading: Extension permitted
children.label: a Extension permitted
children.text: If the value of a reversionary or remainder interest in property is included under chapter 11 in the value of the gross estate, the payment of the part of the tax under chapter 11 attributable to such interest may, at the election of the executor, be postponed until 6 months after the termination of the precedent interest or interests in the property, under such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Extension for reasonable cause
children.label: b Extension for reasonable cause
children.text: At the expiration of the period of postponement provided for in subsection (a), the Secretary may, for reasonable cause, extend the time for payment for a reasonable period or periods not in excess of 3 years from the expiration of the period of postponement provided in subsection (a).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: c Cross reference
children.text: For authority of the Secretary to require security in the case of an extension under this section, see section 6165.
